denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 05/09/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103690254          Group #: GRP-1466
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-167863
Claim #: CLM-2026-212699
Date of Service (requested): 04/19/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 04/21/2026 for Skin biopsy for intraepidermal nerve fiber density related to Small fiber neuropathy, idiopathic (G62.89). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Skin biopsy for intraepidermal nerve fiber density
Diagnosis (as submitted): Small fiber neuropathy, idiopathic (G62.89)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied:
Clinical criteria applied: InterQual criteria (2018) and/or older MCG modules (superseded guideline reference). CPB 0126 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0126 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A less costly modality is appropriate before Skin biopsy for intraepidermal nerve fiber density for Small fiber neuropathy, idiopathic (G62.89). Plan policy requires step-down diagnostic or therapeutic approach.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
Appeals contact (as listed): Appeals Unit, P.O. Box 14582, Lexington, KY 40512-4582; Fax: (877) 555-0199.
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103690254) and reference PA-2026-167863. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 69-year-old member has Small fiber neuropathy, idiopathic (G62.89). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Skin biopsy for intraepidermal nerve fiber density recommended as the next appropriate step. Burning feet; normal large-fiber NCS; autonomic symptoms mild. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that modality substitution bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
